description:
Your repository already contains a small toy project located at

        /home/user/projects/alpha-tool/

    and the following three UTF-8 text files are currently present:

        1. /home/user/projects/alpha-tool/VERSION
               Current single-line content (no trailing spaces):
                   1.2.3

        2. /home/user/projects/alpha-tool/CHANGELOG.md
               Current content:
                   # Changelog

                   ## [1.2.0] - 2023-07-07
                   - Initial public release

        3. /home/user/projects/alpha-tool/TODO.md
               Current content:
                   - Add support for YAML export
                   - Refactor config parser
                   - Improve CLI help text

    Imagine you are the maintainer who is about to release version 1.3.0.  
    Perform the version bump and update the documentation as follows.

    REQUIRED END STATE
    ------------------

    A. /home/user/projects/alpha-tool/VERSION  
       • It must contain exactly the single line “1.3.0” (followed by a newline).

    B. /home/user/projects/alpha-tool/CHANGELOG.md  
       • The file must still start with a level-1 heading “# Changelog”.  
       • Directly underneath, insert a new section that is formatted **exactly** like this
         (including blank lines and spaces):

             ## [1.3.0] - 2023-10-05

             - Add support for YAML export
             - Refactor config parser
             - Improve CLI help text

       • The older section “## [1.2.0] - 2023-07-07” must remain unchanged and
         immediately follow the new section.  
       • Do NOT reorder or re-wrap the bullet points.

    C. /home/user/projects/alpha-tool/TODO.md  
       • After the migration its size must be 0 bytes (an entirely empty file).

    D. /home/user/version_bump.log  
       • Create this new log file in the user’s home directory.  
       • It must consist of exactly one line, no extra blank lines.  
       • The line must match the regular expression (anchors included):

             ^Bumped 1\.2\.3 -> 1\.3\.0 on [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$

         In other words, write the sentence  
             Bumped 1.2.3 -> 1.3.0 on <UTC-timestamp>  
         where the timestamp is obtained from the command  
             date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"

    CONSTRAINTS
    -----------

    • Stay in the “/home/user” directory tree; you have no root privileges.  
    • You may use any standard CLI utilities available in a typical Debian/Ubuntu image.  
    • When editing files, preserve existing line endings (LF) and character encoding (UTF-8).

    SUCCESS CRITERIA (checked by an automated test)
    -----------------------------------------------

    1. VERSION reports “1.3.0”.  
    2. CHANGELOG.md contains the new 1.3.0 section exactly as specified and older
       entries remain intact.  
    3. TODO.md is empty (0 bytes).  
    4. /home/user/version_bump.log exists, is readable, contains exactly one line,
       and that line matches the regex given in Section D above.

    The whole job can be accomplished in roughly 5–10 shell commands.
    Start whenever you are ready.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth expectations for the automated checker -->
    <file path="/home/user/projects/alpha-tool/VERSION">
1.3.0
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/projects/alpha-tool/CHANGELOG.md">
# Changelog

## [1.3.0] - 2023-10-05

- Add support for YAML export
- Refactor config parser
- Improve CLI help text

## [1.2.0] - 2023-07-07
- Initial public release
    </file>

    <!-- TODO.md must be completely empty -->
    <file path="/home/user/projects/alpha-tool/TODO.md">
    </file>

    <!-- The exact timestamp is non-deterministic; the test will use the regex below -->
    <logfile path="/home/user/version_bump.log" regex="^Bumped 1\.2\.3 -> 1\.3\.0 on [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$"/>
name: task_000000_9f315897
